steps.title: Isolate b² by rearranging
steps.content:
Start with the equation:
$$b^2 + 5c = 3d$$
Subtract $5c$ from both sides to isolate the term with $b$:
$$b^2 = 3d - 5c$$

steps.title: Solve for b by taking the square root
steps.content:
Since $b^2 = 3d - 5c$, take the square root of both sides. A square root produces two results (positive and negative):
$$b = \pm\sqrt{3d - 5c}$$
The constraint $d > \frac{5}{3}c$ ensures that $3d > 5c$, so the radicand is positive and real solutions exist.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
